outcome.note: Measured after training the edited schema in the next iteration.

outcome_decision.reason: Task proxy improved enough and hack risk did not increase beyond tolerance.
Task proxy improved enough and hack risk did not increase beyond tolerance.

quality.interpretation: Reusable only when applicability matches; supported by clear proxy improvement.

what_worked: Decreasing per‑step shaping weights effectively reduced episode reward mean and shifted the reward budget.
Adding a sparse milestone event (r_landing_region_once) improved task proxy and reduced hack score.
what_failed: Previous attempt to increase landing bonuses and relax durations was rejected by the gate due to low evidence (zero trigger rate), leaving the agent with only the shaping reduction.
Solo shaping reductions without terminal enhancements did not induce landing behavior.
avoid_next: Do not apply solo shaping reductions without the corresponding terminal enhancements.
Do not let gate logic reject essential complementary edits in a coupled package.
recommend_next: When a coupled rebalancing is required, ensure all parts of the package are applied together, even if some targets currently show low evidence.
After this edit, closely monitor landing event trigger rates and adjust conditions if needed.
agent_lessons.applicability_notes: The shaping‑vs‑terminal rebalancing pattern is general for any landing/contact task where continuous shaping can be exploited.

lesson_card.summary: Coupled rebalancing: increase one‑time landing bonuses and relax duration conditions to make terminal rewards dominant over hovering shaping, shifting agent toward stable landings.
